winner.title: Self-describing proof envelope with content-type and version negotiation
winner.description: Currently proofs are bare JSON arrays of bigints with implicit positional semantics — integrators must know that `publicSignals[0]` is `humanMerkleRoot` and `publicSignals[4]` is `nonceBinding`. Define a `BolyraEnvelope` codec (msgpack or CBOR with JSON fallback) that wraps proofs with `{ version: '0.2', circuit: 'HumanUniqueness', provingSystem: 'groth16', publicSignals: { humanMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, nonceBinding, ... }, proof: {...} }`. Add `BolyraEnvelope.encode()` / `BolyraEnvelope.decode()` to both TS and Python SDKs. This eliminates the `publicSignals[5]` positional indexing that caused the chainId mismatch bug in the cross-chain experiment.

outline.title: Self-describing proof envelope with content-type and version negotiation

artifacts.description: Canonical BolyraEnvelope schema definition: fields, versioning contract, content-type header value (application/bolyra-proof+json and application/bolyra-proof+cbor), and migration notes from bare publicSignals arrays.

BolyraEnvelope TypeScript implementation: CircuitPublicSignals discriminated union types per circuit (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation), encode()/decode() with JSON default and optional msgpack/CBOR via dynamic import, version negotiation helper (negotiateVersion).
Positional index maps for each circuit — converts legacy bare publicSignals arrays to named-field objects and back. Single source of truth replacing ad-hoc indexing throughout the SDK.
Python BolyraEnvelope dataclass with encode()/decode() methods. JSON codec is pure-Python; CBOR codec uses cbor2 (optional dependency). Mirrors TS named-field schema for interoperability.
Re-export BolyraEnvelope, CircuitPublicSignals types, and negotiateVersion from public API surface.

Unit tests: round-trip encode/decode for each circuit type in JSON and msgpack; backward-compat decode of legacy bare-array proofs; version negotiation edge cases; schema validation rejection of unknown fields.
Python mirror of TS envelope tests: round-trip JSON and CBOR, cross-SDK interop fixture (encode in TS, decode in Python via shared JSON fixture file), rejection cases.
Shared JSON fixture containing pre-encoded envelopes for each circuit/codec combination used by both TS and Python cross-SDK interop tests.
Migration guide for integrators: before/after code snippets, backward-compat window policy, how to detect envelope version at runtime, content-type negotiation in HTTP contexts.

steps: Define BolyraEnvelope schema in spec/proof-envelope-v1.md: top-level fields (version, circuit, provingSystem, publicSignals as named object, proof), allowed version strings ('0.2', future '0.3'), content-type values, and backward-compat rules for bare-array inputs.

Author sdk/src/circuits/signal-maps.ts: define PositionalMap<T> type and one map per circuit (HumanUniqueness: [humanMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, nonceBinding, ...], AgentPolicy, Delegation). Export fromArray(signals, map) and toArray(named, map) converters.

Implement sdk/src/envelope.ts: BolyraEnvelope<C extends CircuitName> generic interface, encode() serializes to JSON (default) or msgpack (via dynamic import of @msgpack/msgpack), decode() accepts Buffer/string and detects codec from leading byte or explicit codec param, negotiateVersion(offered[], supported[]) picks highest mutually supported version.
Update sdk/src/index.ts to export BolyraEnvelope, CircuitPublicSignals union, negotiateVersion, and signal-maps utilities.

Refactor existing SDK call sites (proveHandshake, verifyHandshake, createAgentCredential) to produce/consume BolyraEnvelope internally while keeping legacy bare-array output behind a deprecated flag for one version cycle.

Implement sdk-python/bolyra/envelope.py: BolyraEnvelopeCodec dataclass with encode()/decode() supporting 'json' and 'cbor' (cbor2) backends; mirror the named-signal field names from signal-maps.ts exactly.
Generate sdk/test/fixtures/envelope-interop.json by running the TS encoder against known witness inputs for each circuit; commit as a static fixture.
Write sdk/test/envelope.test.ts covering: round-trip per codec, legacy bare-array decode, cross-version negotiation, unknown-field rejection.
Write sdk-python/tests/test_envelope.py covering: round-trip JSON and CBOR, decode of interop fixture produced by TS encoder, rejection of malformed envelopes.
Add cbor2 as an optional dependency in sdk-python/pyproject.toml under [project.optional-dependencies] (extras = ['cbor']).
Update docs/proof-envelope-migration.md with before/after snippets showing publicSignals[0] → envelope.publicSignals.humanMerkleRoot, HTTP Content-Type header usage, and deprecation timeline.
Run npm test and pytest -v; confirm all existing circuit and contract tests still pass with refactored call sites.
dependencies: @msgpack/msgpack (optional, dynamic import in TS SDK)
cbor2 (optional Python extra)
Existing snarkjs proof output shape (groth16: {pi_a, pi_b, pi_c, protocol, curve})
Signal index order from circuits/src/HumanUniqueness.circom, AgentPolicy.circom, Delegation.circom output signal declarations
TS SDK version bump to 0.3.0 (semver minor — additive public API change)
Python SDK version bump to 0.6.0

winner.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors for all three circuits
winner.description: Create spec/test-vectors/ with JSON files covering: valid handshake (human+agent), expired agent credential, stale Merkle root, scope subset violation, cumulative bit encoding violation, delegation chain (1 and 3 hops), nonce replay, and revoked identity. Each vector includes named inputs, expected witness outputs or expected failure reason. A conformance runner (spec/conformance-runner.js) loads vectors and runs them against the circuit witness generator. This is the minimum bar for any alternative implementation to prove compatibility.

outline.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors for all three circuits

artifacts.description: Valid human+agent handshake with all required inputs and expected public outputs
Agent credential with expiry < current block timestamp; expects witness failure
HumanUniqueness proof against a root not in the on-chain registry; expects verification failure
Delegation circuit input where delegated permissions exceed delegator's scope; expects constraint failure
AgentPolicy input with bit encoding violating implication rules (e.g. bit 3 set without bit 2); expects validateCumulativeBitEncoding failure
Single-hop delegation chain: operator -> agent with narrowed permissions; expects valid proof
Three-hop delegation chain with monotonically narrowing permissions at each hop; expects valid proof
Reused sessionNonce on a second handshake with same (humanProof, agentProof); expects nullifier clash or verification failure
Human identity whose nullifierHash appears in the on-chain revocation set; expects registry check failure
JSON Schema defining the test vector envelope: id, circuit, inputs, expected_outputs, expected_failure, notes fields
Node.js runner that loads all vectors from spec/test-vectors/, invokes the circuit witness generator for each, asserts outputs or failure reason, and emits a TAP-compatible report
Authoring guide: how to add new vectors, what fields are required, how to run the conformance suite, and what passing means for alternative implementations

steps: Define the JSON Schema (spec/test-vectors/schema.json) covering: id, circuit (HumanUniqueness|AgentPolicy|Delegation), inputs (named signal map), expected_outputs (public signal map, nullable), expected_failure (string reason or null), and notes
Author valid_handshake.json using real signal names from HumanUniqueness.circom and AgentPolicy.circom; derive expected public outputs by running the witness generator once and capturing results
Author expired_agent_credential.json with expiry set to block 0 and current_time > 0; mark expected_failure as 'expiry_constraint'
Author stale_merkle_root.json with a root value not matching any leaf path in the test Merkle tree; mark expected_failure as 'merkle_root_mismatch'
Author scope_subset_violation.json for Delegation.circom with delegatedPermissions bits strictly superset of delegatorPermissions; mark expected_failure as 'scope_expansion_constraint'
Author cumulative_bit_violation.json for AgentPolicy.circom with permissions=0b00001000 (bit 3 set, bit 2 clear); mark expected_failure as 'cumulative_bit_constraint'
Author delegation_1hop.json: single Delegation circuit invocation with operator key, agent pubkey, and narrowed 4-bit permission mask; provide expected valid proof public outputs
Author delegation_3hop.json: compose three Delegation circuit invocations, each narrowing permissions by one bit; verify final delegated permissions equal intersection of all three masks
Author nonce_replay.json: identical (humanNullifier, agentCommitment, sessionNonce) tuple used twice; mark expected_failure as 'nullifier_replay'
Author revoked_identity.json: nullifierHash present in a mock revocation Merkle set; mark expected_failure as 'identity_revoked'

Implement spec/conformance-runner.js: parse all *.json files (excluding schema.json) in spec/test-vectors/, for each vector load the matching circuit's wasm/zkey from circuits/build/, call snarkjs.wtns.calculate(), compare outputs or catch expected constraint errors, emit TAP lines
Add a 'test:conformance' npm script in package.json pointing to node spec/conformance-runner.js
Wire test:conformance into CI (GitHub Actions) alongside test:circuits:fast so it runs on every PR
Write spec/CONFORMANCE.md documenting the vector schema, how to generate witness baselines, and the interpretation of expected_failure strings for alternative implementors
dependencies: circuits/build/HumanUniqueness.wasm
circuits/build/AgentPolicy.wasm
circuits/build/Delegation.wasm
circuits/build/HumanUniqueness_final.zkey
circuits/build/AgentPolicy_final.zkey
circuits/build/Delegation_final.zkey
snarkjs
circuits/src/HumanUniqueness.circom
circuits/src/AgentPolicy.circom
circuits/src/Delegation.circom
circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md

winner.title: Add human tree root history buffer matching agent tree's 30-root buffer
winner.description: IdentityRegistry maintains a 30-root history buffer for agentTree but humanTree has no such buffer — any new enrollment immediately invalidates in-flight human proofs. This is an interop hazard: a compliant verifier cannot know whether a human proof was generated against a recently-valid root. Add the same ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 ring buffer for humanTree roots, with an isKnownHumanRoot() view function. Update the IETF draft's Security Considerations to document the root staleness window.

outline.title: Add human tree root history buffer matching agent tree's 30-root buffer

artifacts.description: Add ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE=30 ring buffer for humanTree roots: uint256[30] humanRootHistory, uint32 humanRootIndex counter, populate on each enrollment in _insertHumanLeaf, add isKnownHumanRoot(uint256 root) view function mirroring existing isKnownAgentRoot logic
Expose isKnownHumanRoot(uint256 root) external view in the interface so verifier contracts can call it without knowing the implementation
Update verifyHumanProof() to call isKnownHumanRoot(publicInputs.humanMerkleRoot) instead of requiring an exact current-root match, accepting any root within the history buffer

Hardhat tests: enroll 31 humans and assert root #0 is evicted, that isKnownHumanRoot returns true for roots 1-30 and false for root 0; assert in-flight proof generated before enrollment N+1 still verifies until it falls out of the 30-slot window

Integration test: generate a human proof against root R, advance the tree 5 times, confirm proof still verifies; advance 30 more times, confirm proof now fails — validates the staleness window boundary

Add Security Considerations subsection 'Human Merkle Root Staleness Window': document that humanTree maintains a 30-root ring buffer, that verifiers MUST call isKnownHumanRoot rather than comparing against current root, and that proofs older than 30 enrollment events are irrevocably invalid

Note under replay-attack mitigations that human proofs carry an implicit TTL bounded by enrollment velocity × 30 slots, and that high-throughput deployments should set ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE higher at deploy time

steps: Read contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol to locate existing agentTree root buffer pattern (agentRootHistory array, agentRootIndex counter, isKnownAgentRoot view)
Add parallel storage variables to IdentityRegistry: `uint256[ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE] public humanRootHistory` and `uint32 public humanRootIndex`
In _insertHumanLeaf (or equivalent enrollment internal): after updating humanTree root, write new root into humanRootHistory[humanRootIndex % ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE] and increment humanRootIndex
Implement `isKnownHumanRoot(uint256 root) public view returns (bool)` iterating over humanRootHistory exactly as isKnownAgentRoot does
Add isKnownHumanRoot to IIdentityRegistry.sol interface
Update HumanUniquenessVerifier.verifyHumanProof() to replace exact-root equality check with IIdentityRegistry(registry).isKnownHumanRoot(publicInputs.humanMerkleRoot)

Write unit tests in IdentityRegistry.humanRootHistory.test.ts covering: first enrollment populates slot 0, 30th enrollment fills buffer, 31st enrollment wraps and evicts slot 0, isKnownHumanRoot returns correct boolean for all cases
Write integration test in HumanUniquenessVerifier.integration.test.ts proving the staleness boundary at exactly 30 enrollments
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md Security Considerations with root staleness window documentation
Update docs/owasp-agentic-mapping.md replay-attack section
Run `npm run test:contracts` and confirm all existing + new tests pass
Sign commit with DCO: `git commit -s -m 'feat(contracts): add 30-root history buffer for humanTree matching agentTree parity'`
dependencies: contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol — must locate existing agentTree buffer pattern before mirroring
contracts/src/interfaces/IIdentityRegistry.sol — interface must be updated before verifier can reference isKnownHumanRoot
HumanUniquenessVerifier.sol depends on IIdentityRegistry.isKnownHumanRoot being defined first
Integration tests depend on compiled contracts and a deployed IdentityRegistry fixture
spec update is editorial-only and can proceed in parallel with contract changes

winner.title: Add blinding salt to scope commitment to prevent brute-force
winner.description: scopeCommitment = Poseidon2(bitmask, credCommitment) is deterministic over a 256-value bitmask space (8 bits). An observer can precompute all 256 possible commitments for a known credCommitment and determine the exact permission set. Change to Poseidon3(bitmask, credCommitment, blindingSalt) where blindingSalt is a private random field element. Update AgentPolicy, Delegation circuits, SDK proof generation, and the IETF draft's privacy analysis section. This is a protocol-level privacy fix that must happen before any production deployment.

outline.title: Add blinding salt to scope commitment to prevent brute-force

artifacts.description: Change scopeCommitment from Poseidon2(bitmask, credCommitment) to Poseidon3(bitmask, credCommitment, blindingSalt). Add blindingSalt as a private input signal.

Propagate blindingSalt as a private input; update scopeCommitment computation to Poseidon3(bitmask, credCommitment, blindingSalt). Verify narrowed-scope commitment uses a fresh delegateSalt, not the parent salt.

Add witness-generation tests for blinded commitment. Assert that two proofs with identical bitmask + credCommitment but different blindingSalt produce distinct scopeCommitments. Add negative test: replaying a commitment with wrong salt must fail.
Add tests verifying delegateSalt independence from parent salt, and that delegation still enforces one-way scope narrowing under blinded commitments.
Generate cryptographically random blindingSalt (32-byte field element) during createAgentCredential(); include it in the AgentCredential type as a private field. Pass to prover inputs.
Extend AgentCredential interface with blindingSalt: bigint (private, never serialized in public credential objects). Extend ProverInputs accordingly.
Thread blindingSalt through proveHandshake() prover input assembly. Ensure salt is never included in the public proof JSON or handshake transcript.
Add blinding_salt field to AgentCredential dataclass (private, not exported). Update subprocess bridge call to include salt in prover args.
Regenerate Solidity verifier from updated AgentPolicy.zkey after circuit change. No semantic changes required; file is auto-generated.
Regenerate Solidity verifier from updated Delegation.zkey after circuit change.

Update Section on scopeCommitment construction to specify Poseidon3(bitmask, credCommitment, blindingSalt). Add privacy analysis subsection: brute-force precomputation attack on 8-bit bitmask, why salt closes it, and that blindingSalt MUST be generated fresh per credential issuance using a CSPRNG with at least 128-bit entropy.
Note in the credential representation section that blindingSalt is a private holder secret excluded from DID Document and VC proof objects.
Update permission scope privacy entry: scope commitment is now unlinkable across sessions even when credCommitment is known to an observer.
Hardhat integration test: submit blinded-scope proof on-chain; assert verifyProof returns true. Confirm on-chain verifier rejects a proof built from pre-salt commitment under the new verifier.

steps: Add blindingSalt as a private input to AgentPolicy.circom; replace Poseidon2 call with Poseidon3(bitmask, credCommitment, blindingSalt); verify constraint count delta is +1 Poseidon3 gate (~240 constraints).
Mirror the same change in Delegation.circom, using a separate delegateSalt private input so parent and child commitments are independently blinded and unlinkable.
Recompile both circuits: npm run compile:circuits; confirm .r1cs, .zkey, and .vkey artifacts are regenerated cleanly.
Regenerate Solidity verifiers from new vkey.json for both AgentPolicy and Delegation; replace contracts/src/AgentPolicyVerifier.sol and DelegationVerifier.sol.

Extend AgentCredential TypeScript type with blindingSalt: bigint (private). Update createAgentCredential() to generate a random 32-byte field element via crypto.getRandomValues and reduce mod BN254 scalar field order.
Update proveHandshake() and prover input assembly in sdk/src/handshake.ts to pass blindingSalt; audit all proof serialization paths to confirm salt is stripped from public output.
Mirror salt field in sdk-python AgentCredential dataclass; update subprocess bridge JSON payload to include blinding_salt key.

Write fast circuit tests (witness-only) for AgentPolicy and Delegation covering: (a) distinct salts produce distinct commitments, (b) replayed commitment with wrong salt rejected, (c) delegation scope-narrowing still holds under blinding.
Write Hardhat integration tests submitting blinded proofs against regenerated on-chain verifiers.
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md with new scopeCommitment formula and privacy analysis subsection documenting the brute-force attack and mitigation.
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md and docs/owasp-agentic-mapping.md to reflect blinded commitment privacy properties.
Run full test suite (npm test) including contracts; confirm all tests pass before cutting a protocol version bump.
dependencies: circomlib Poseidon3 template (already available in circomlib >=2.0.5)
pot16.ptau — existing universal SRS covers the constraint budget for both updated circuits
snarkjs trusted setup scripts for AgentPolicy and Delegation (project-specific phase-2)
BN254 scalar field order constant in SDK for salt reduction
crypto.getRandomValues or Node crypto.randomBytes available in SDK runtime

winner.title: Zero-config quickstart: 5 lines from npm install to verified handshake
winner.description: The current `proveHandshake` requires the caller to resolve WASM/zkey paths, construct Merkle proof objects, and manage nonce generation. Add a `BolyraClient` class with `new BolyraClient({ provider })` that auto-discovers circuit artifacts (embedded via `postinstall` or resolved from `@bolyra/artifacts`), fetches Merkle proofs from the on-chain registry, generates session nonces, and exposes `client.handshake(humanSecret, agentCredential)` returning a verified result. Target: `npm install @bolyra/sdk && const result = await new BolyraClient({provider}).handshake(secret, cred)` — 5 lines including import.

outline.title: Zero-config quickstart: 5 lines from npm install to verified handshake

artifacts.description: BolyraClient class exposing new BolyraClient({ provider }) and client.handshake(humanSecret, agentCredential) — orchestrates artifact resolution, Merkle fetch, nonce gen, prove, and verify in one call
ArtifactResolver: resolves WASM and zkey paths relative to the installed npm package directory (require.resolve('@bolyra/sdk/circuits/...')), with fallback to BOLYRA_ARTIFACTS_DIR env var
MerkleProofFetcher: queries the on-chain HumanRegistry contract via the caller-supplied ethers/viem provider to fetch the current Merkle root and sibling path for a given identity commitment
generateSessionNonce(): crypto.randomBytes(32) wrapped as a typed SessionNonce — single-use, not replayable
Re-export BolyraClient alongside existing public API symbols; bump minor version to 0.3.0
Integration test: stubs ArtifactResolver and MerkleProofFetcher, exercises handshake() end-to-end with mock proofs; separate slow-path test with FULL_PROOF=1 against compiled artifacts
Unit test: verifies ArtifactResolver finds the correct WASM/zkey paths in the local build, and that a missing artifact throws ArtifactNotFoundError with an actionable message
Rewrite opening section to show the 5-line pattern; retain advanced section documenting the low-level proveHandshake/verifyHandshake API for callers who need custom path control

steps: Add circuit WASM and zkey files as package-included assets in sdk/package.json `files` array (or symlink from circuits/build/ via postinstall script) so require.resolve can locate them after npm install

Implement ArtifactResolver (sdk/src/artifacts.ts): check BOLYRA_ARTIFACTS_DIR env first, then resolve via require.resolve('@bolyra/sdk') dirname traversal, throw ArtifactNotFoundError with npm install hint on miss
Implement generateSessionNonce (sdk/src/nonce.ts): crypto.randomBytes(32), return as branded SessionNonce type so callers cannot pass an arbitrary Buffer

Implement MerkleProofFetcher (sdk/src/merkle.ts): accept an ethers Provider or viem PublicClient (union type), call HumanRegistry.getMerkleProof(identityCommitment), return typed MerkleProof; cache root per block to avoid redundant RPC calls

Implement BolyraClient class (sdk/src/client.ts): constructor accepts { provider, artifactsDir? }; handshake(humanSecret, agentCredential) method: (a) derive identity commitment from humanSecret, (b) call MerkleProofFetcher, (c) call generateSessionNonce, (d) call existing proveHandshake with resolved paths, (e) call existing verifyHandshake, (f) return { verified: boolean, nullifierHash, sessionNonce }
Export BolyraClient and companion types (ArtifactNotFoundError, SessionNonce, HandshakeResult) from sdk/src/index.ts; keep existing exports untouched
Write unit tests (sdk/test/artifacts.test.ts) for ArtifactResolver path logic with real filesystem fixtures
Write integration tests (sdk/test/client.test.ts): mock-proof fast path using sinon stubs on ArtifactResolver and MerkleProofFetcher; guard slow real-proof path behind FULL_PROOF=1
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with the 5-line example, provider setup note (ethers.js or viem), and link to advanced API docs
Bump sdk/package.json version to 0.3.0, run tsc --noEmit, npm run build, confirm artifact paths resolve in the built dist/
dependencies: circuits/build/HumanUniqueness.wasm and HumanUniqueness_final.zkey must exist (npm run compile:circuits)
contracts/HumanRegistry deployed address and ABI available in sdk/src/contracts/ (already generated by Hardhat typechain)
ethers v6 or viem already peer-dep of SDK (confirm in sdk/package.json before introducing union provider type)
Existing proveHandshake and verifyHandshake low-level API in sdk/src/index.ts (no changes to those signatures)
Node 18+ crypto.randomBytes available (no additional dep needed for nonce)

winner.title: Typed error hierarchy with machine-readable codes for SDK and contracts
winner.description: The current SDK throws generic `new Error(string)` and the IdentityRegistry uses unparameterized custom errors (e.g., `StaleAgentRoot()` with no context). Replace SDK errors with a typed `BolyraError` class hierarchy carrying `code` (enum like `STALE_ROOT`, `NONCE_MISMATCH`, `SCOPE_SUBSET_VIOLATION`), `context` object, and human-readable `message`. On the Solidity side, add indexed parameters to custom errors (e.g., `StaleAgentRoot(uint256 providedRoot, uint256 latestRoot)`). Publish the error code enum in both `@bolyra/sdk` and `bolyra` Python package so integrators can programmatically catch and recover from specific failure modes without string-parsing.

outline.title: Typed error hierarchy with machine-readable codes for SDK and contracts

artifacts.description: BolyraErrorCode enum and BolyraError base class with code, context, and message fields; subclasses: StaleRootError, NonceMismatchError, ScopeSubsetViolationError, CredentialExpiredError, InvalidProofError, DIDResolutionError
Re-export BolyraError, BolyraErrorCode, and all subclasses from public API surface
Replace generic Error throws with typed BolyraError subclasses carrying structured context objects (nonces, roots, permission bitmasks)
Replace generic Error throws in credential creation/delegation paths with typed errors

Add indexed parameters to all custom errors: StaleAgentRoot(uint256 providedRoot, uint256 latestRoot), NonceMismatch(bytes32 expected, bytes32 actual), ScopeSubsetViolation(uint8 delegatedBits, uint8 parentBits), CredentialExpired(uint256 expiry, uint256 blockTimestamp)
BolyraErrorCode IntEnum mirroring TS enum values; BolyraError base exception with code and context dict; subclasses matching TS hierarchy for programmatic catch in Python integrations
Export BolyraError, BolyraErrorCode, and subclasses from package public API
Canonical error code registry: numeric value, symbolic name, triggering condition, context fields, recovery guidance for each code
Unit tests: each BolyraError subclass carries correct code and context fields; verify integration paths throw typed errors on invalid inputs
Hardhat tests asserting revert with parameterized error payloads using ethers.js custom error decoding; assert providedRoot, latestRoot etc. in revert data
Pytest: BolyraError subclasses are catchable as BolyraError; code enum values match TS counterparts; context dict populated on throw

steps: Audit all `throw new Error(...)` sites in sdk/src/ and all custom error definitions in contracts/src/ to build the complete error inventory

Define BolyraErrorCode numeric enum in sdk/src/errors.ts (STALE_ROOT=1, NONCE_MISMATCH=2, SCOPE_SUBSET_VIOLATION=3, CREDENTIAL_EXPIRED=4, INVALID_PROOF=5, DID_RESOLUTION_FAILED=6, INVALID_PERMISSIONS=7)
Implement BolyraError base class extending Error with readonly fields: code: BolyraErrorCode, context: Record<string, unknown>, and override name/message for structured logging

Implement typed subclasses (StaleRootError, NonceMismatchError, ScopeSubsetViolationError, CredentialExpiredError, InvalidProofError) each accepting strongly-typed context params and calling super with code + serialized context

Replace all generic throws in sdk/src/verify.ts, credential.ts, handshake.ts with the appropriate typed subclass, passing relevant context values (actual vs expected nonce, provided vs latest root, bitmasks)

Add parameterized custom errors to IdentityRegistry.sol: replace bare `StaleAgentRoot()` etc. with `StaleAgentRoot(uint256 providedRoot, uint256 latestRoot)` and update all revert sites to pass values
Regenerate TypeScript ABI bindings (typechain) for contracts so SDK can decode parameterized revert data from on-chain calls into BolyraError instances
Mirror BolyraErrorCode as IntEnum in sdk-python/bolyra/errors.py using the same numeric values as the TS enum; implement BolyraError(Exception) base and subclasses
Update subprocess bridge in sdk-python to parse structured error payloads from JS process stderr/stdout and re-raise as Python BolyraError subclasses
Write spec/error-codes.md: table with code value, name, triggering condition, context field schema, and recovery guidance
Write tests: sdk/test/errors.test.ts (unit), contracts/test/IdentityRegistryErrors.test.ts (Hardhat integration with custom error decoding), sdk-python/tests/test_errors.py
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md and sdk-python/README.md with error handling examples showing programmatic catch by code
Run full test suite (npm test + pytest) and verify no generic Error leaks remain in the public-facing code paths
dependencies: @bolyra/sdk current throw sites audit
contracts/src/IdentityRegistry.sol custom error definitions
typechain or ethers.js v6 custom error ABI decoding support
sdk-python subprocess bridge (sdk-python/bolyra/bridge.py or equivalent)
spec/did-method-bolyra.md for DIDResolutionError context field definitions

winner.title: Fluent delegation chain builder with compile-time scope narrowing
winner.description: The existing delegation API requires manually constructing Merkle proofs, scope bitmasks, and chaining `previousScopeCommitment` across hops — a footgun-rich surface. Build a `DelegationChain.from(agentCredential).narrowScope(Permission.FINANCIAL_SMALL | Permission.READ_DATA).delegateTo(delegateeCredential).build()` fluent builder that validates cumulative bit encoding at construction time (TypeScript generics prevent expanding scope), auto-fetches Merkle proofs from the registry, and serializes the chain into the wire format the contract expects. The builder produces a `DelegationChainProof` that `verifyHandshake` already knows how to consume.

outline.title: Fluent delegation chain builder with compile-time scope narrowing

artifacts.description: Branded numeric type `PermissionSet<N extends number>` with `narrowTo<M extends N>` that enforces M ⊆ N at compile time via conditional types; exports `Permission` const enum for all 8 bits.

Fluent builder class: `from(agentCredential)` seeds root scope, `narrowScope(permissions)` emits a compile-time error when expanding, `delegateTo(delegateeCredential)` appends a hop and chains `previousScopeCommitment`, `build()` resolves Merkle proofs and serializes the `DelegationChainProof` wire object.
Async fetcher that queries the on-chain registry (via ethers provider) for Merkle proofs keyed by credential commitment hash; result is cached per session to avoid redundant RPC calls.

Wire-format type `DelegationChainProof` with `hops: DelegationHop[]`, each carrying `groth16Proof`, `publicSignals`, `scopeCommitment`, `nullifierHash`, and `delegateeCommitment`; includes a `serialize()` method for ABI encoding.
Re-exports `DelegationChain`, `Permission`, `PermissionSet`, and `DelegationChainProof` as the public delegation API surface.
Add `DelegationChain` and related exports to the top-level SDK barrel; bump to v0.4.0 reflecting new public API.

Verify (or extend if needed) that the existing Delegation circuit accepts `previousScopeCommitment` as a public input and enforces the cumulative-bit subset constraint on-chain; no new circuit if invariants already hold.
Add `getCommitmentProof(bytes32 commitment) returns (bytes32[] memory path, uint256 index)` view function so the SDK fetcher can pull Merkle proofs without a separate indexer.

Unit tests: single-hop narrowing succeeds, multi-hop chain accumulates commitments correctly, scope expansion rejected at compile time (tsd type assertion), invalid cumulative encoding throws at runtime, Merkle proof fetch is mocked via ethers stub.
Integration test against local Hardhat node: builds a 2-hop chain, submits to `verifyHandshake`, asserts on-chain verification passes; uses `FULL_PROOF=1` flag to generate real Groth16 proofs.
Add section 6.4 'Delegation Chain Wire Format' describing `DelegationChainProof` structure, hop ordering, and how `verifyHandshake` consumes the chain.
Add 'Delegating Credentials' section with end-to-end fluent builder example, Permission enum table, and note on compile-time scope narrowing guarantees.

steps: Audit existing Delegation.circom to confirm `previousScopeCommitment` is already a public input and the cumulative-bit subset constraint is enforced; document any gaps.
Add `getCommitmentProof` view function to DelegationRegistry.sol; regenerate ABI and TypeChain bindings; write Hardhat unit test for the new view.
Define `Permission` const enum and `PermissionSet<N>` branded type in PermissionSet.ts; write tsd compile-time assertions that narrowing compiles and expansion fails.
Implement `MerkleProofFetcher` with ethers provider injection, keyed by commitment hash, session-scoped LRU cache.
Implement `DelegationChainProof` wire type with `serialize()` producing ABI-encoded bytes matching what `verifyHandshake` expects.

Implement `DelegationChain` fluent builder: `from()` captures root AgentCredential and its scope, `narrowScope()` validates subset at both compile time (conditional type) and runtime (`validateCumulativeBitEncoding`), `delegateTo()` appends a hop and links `previousScopeCommitment`, `build()` awaits Merkle proof fetches and invokes the Delegation circuit prover.
Wire builder into the existing `proveHandshake` / `verifyHandshake` flow so `DelegationChainProof` is consumed transparently when present.
Write unit tests with mocked ethers provider and mocked snarkjs prover; assert chain structure and error conditions.
Write integration test on local Hardhat node with real Groth16 proofs gated behind FULL_PROOF=1.
Update spec section 6.4 with wire format and hop-ordering semantics.
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with delegation builder example.
Bump SDK to v0.4.0, update CHANGELOG, run `tsc --noEmit` and full test suite, verify DCO sign-off on all commits.
dependencies: Delegation.circom with previousScopeCommitment public input
DelegationRegistry.sol on-chain Merkle commitment storage
validateCumulativeBitEncoding() in sdk/src/permissions.ts
snarkjs Groth16 prover (dev/test) and rapidsnark_prover binary (production)
ethers v6 provider for registry RPC calls
TypeScript 5.x (conditional types for compile-time scope narrowing)
tsd for compile-time type assertion tests
Hardhat local node for integration tests
pot16.ptau + Delegation.zkey build artifacts

winner.title: Off-chain session token from verified handshake
winner.description: After a successful on-chain handshake verification, emit a signed JWT (or SD-JWT) session token that encodes the verified scope, nullifiers, and expiry. Downstream tool calls can present this token instead of re-proving on-chain, enabling sub-100ms auth for agentic workflows. The token references the on-chain transaction hash as its trust anchor. This is the missing bridge between ZKP correctness and real-time agentic commerce latency requirements — without it, every tool call requires a fresh proof or an on-chain transaction, making Bolyra unusable for high-frequency agent-to-agent interactions.

outline.title: Off-chain session token from verified handshake

artifacts.description: Session token specification: JWT/SD-JWT claim schema, trust anchor (txHash), nullifier binding, scope encoding, expiry policy, and revocation semantics.

issueSessionToken(verifiedHandshake, signingKey, opts): produces a signed SD-JWT encoding nullifierHash, humanMerkleRoot, agentPermissions bitmap, txHash, iat, exp. Also exports verifySessionToken(token, verifyingKey) for downstream fast-path auth.
SessionTokenPayload interface: nullifierHash, humanMerkleRoot, agentPermissions, txHash, sessionNonce, iat, exp, iss. Extend VerifyHandshakeResult to carry txHash.
Python mirror: issue_session_token() and verify_session_token() calling the JS subprocess bridge. Thin wrapper — no re-implementation of crypto.
Unit tests: happy-path issuance after mock verifyHandshake, tampered-payload rejection, expired token rejection, permission-bitmap round-trip, txHash claim presence.
Python-side tests: issue → verify round-trip via subprocess bridge, expired token, missing txHash.
Usage guide: how to issue after verifyHandshake, present to downstream tool calls, validate latency profile, token lifetime recommendations, and revocation via nullifier registry.

steps: Define SessionTokenPayload schema in spec/session-token-bolyra.md: mandatory claims (nullifierHash, humanMerkleRoot, agentPermissions, txHash, sessionNonce, iat, exp, iss), optional SD-JWT disclosures for individual permission bits.
Extend VerifyHandshakeResult in sdk/src/types.ts to include txHash (on-chain transaction hash returned by the verifier contract call).
Implement issueSessionToken() in sdk/src/session.ts using jose (ES256 or EdDSA): pack payload, sign with operator signing key, return compact SD-JWT string.
Implement verifySessionToken() in sdk/src/session.ts: verify signature, check exp, assert txHash non-empty, decode agentPermissions bitmap, return typed SessionTokenPayload.
Add selective-disclosure layer: wrap agentPermissions individual bits as SD-JWT disclosures so downstream tools receive only the scope they need, not the full bitmap.
Wire issueSessionToken into the public SDK API (sdk/src/index.ts exports) and update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with a two-line example.
Implement Python bridge in sdk-python/bolyra/session.py: call JS SDK via existing subprocess bridge pattern, parse returned JSON, expose typed dataclass.
Write TypeScript tests (sdk/test/session.test.ts): happy path, tampered payload, expired, wrong key, missing txHash, permission bitmap round-trip.
Write Python tests (sdk-python/tests/test_session.py): round-trip via subprocess, error propagation.
Run full test suite (npm test + pytest -v), fix any failures, then verify sub-100ms cold-path verification with a quick benchmark in the test output.
Add docs/session-tokens.md: issuance flow diagram, lifetime guidance (recommend ≤15 min for agentic sessions), revocation note (nullifier blacklist on verifier contract).
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md to reference the session token as the fast-path auth layer in Section 5 (Protocol Flow).
dependencies: verifyHandshake() must return the on-chain txHash from the verifier contract call
jose library (npm) for JWT/SD-JWT signing — already a common peer dep, confirm it is present or add to sdk/package.json
sd-jwt-vc or @sd-jwt/core for selective disclosure layer (evaluate against jose's built-in SD-JWT support in jose >=5.x)
Operator signing key management: caller must supply an ES256 or EdDSA private key; spec must define key registration / rotation semantics
On-chain nullifier registry (contracts/) must be queryable for revocation checks — tie to existing BolyraRegistry if present
Python subprocess bridge (sdk-python/bolyra/_bridge.py or equivalent) must be stable before Python session module is added
